title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants two changes: (1) Fix the CEL validation rule on `PolicyOverrides` to use `size(self.rules) > 0` instead of `self.rules != null`, because the current rule lets empty maps (`{}`) pass the CRD check while the webhook rejects themâ€”a problem if the webhook is unreachable. (2) Add `// +kubebuilder:object:generate=false` above the `KeystoneWebhook` struct to stop controller-gen from generating unnecessary deepcopy methods for a zero-field utility struct.

code_comments.body:
The CEL rule `self.rules != null` evaluates to **true** when `rules` is an empty map (`{}`), because an empty map is not null. However, the webhook (keystone_webhook.go line 114) rejects this case via `len(k.Spec.PolicyOverrides.Rules) == 0 && ConfigMapRef == nil`.

This means:
- A `policyOverrides: {rules: {}}` payload **passes** the CRD schema check (XValidation), but
- **fails** the webhook validation.

If the webhook server is ever unreachable or not yet registered, the XValidation rule is the only gate â€” and it will silently allow empty `rules` through. The CEL expression should mirror the webhook's semantic precisely:

```suggestion
	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="size(self.rules) > 0 || self.configMapRef != null",message="at least one of rules or configMapRef must be set"
	PolicyOverrides *commonv1.PolicySpec `json:"policyOverrides,omitempty"`
```

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go
Line: 77-78

Comment:
The CEL rule `self.rules != null` evaluates to **true** when `rules` is an empty map (`{}`), because an empty map is not null. However, the webhook (keystone_webhook.go line 114) rejects this case via `len(k.Spec.PolicyOverrides.Rules) == 0 && ConfigMapRef == nil`.

This means:
- A `policyOverrides: {rules: {}}` payload **passes** the CRD schema check (XValidation), but
- **fails** the webhook validation.

If the webhook server is ever unreachable or not yet registered, the XValidation rule is the only gate â€” and it will silently allow empty `rules` through. The CEL expression should mirror the webhook's semantic precisely:

```suggestion
	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="size(self.rules) > 0 || self.configMapRef != null",message="at least one of rules or configMapRef must be set"
	PolicyOverrides *commonv1.PolicySpec `json:"policyOverrides,omitempty"`
```

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Changed the CEL XValidation rule on PolicyOverrides (keystone_types.go:77) from 'self.rules != null || self.configMapRef != null' to 'size(self.rules) > 0 || self.configMapRef != null', so an empty rules map ({}) is now correctly rejected at the CRD schema level, matching the webhook validation logic.

code_comments.body:
The `+kubebuilder:object:generate=true` package-level marker in `groupversion_info.go` causes controller-gen to emit `DeepCopyInto`/`DeepCopy` for every struct in the package, including `KeystoneWebhook{}`. `KeystoneWebhook` is a zero-field utility struct, not a Kubernetes runtime object, so generating deepcopy for it is unnecessary noise in the generated file.

Add the per-type opt-out marker directly above the struct declaration in `keystone_webhook.go`:

```go
// +kubebuilder:object:generate=false
type KeystoneWebhook struct{}
```

Re-running `make generate` will drop the two methods from `zz_generated.deepcopy.go`.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go
Line: 207-218

Comment:
The `+kubebuilder:object:generate=true` package-level marker in `groupversion_info.go` causes controller-gen to emit `DeepCopyInto`/`DeepCopy` for every struct in the package, including `KeystoneWebhook{}`. `KeystoneWebhook` is a zero-field utility struct, not a Kubernetes runtime object, so generating deepcopy for it is unnecessary noise in the generated file.

Add the per-type opt-out marker directly above the struct declaration in `keystone_webhook.go`:

```go
// +kubebuilder:object:generate=false
type KeystoneWebhook struct{}
```

Re-running `make generate` will drop the two methods from `zz_generated.deepcopy.go`.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added '// +kubebuilder:object:generate=false' marker above the KeystoneWebhook struct in keystone_webhook.go and re-ran controller-gen, which removed the spurious DeepCopyInto and DeepCopy methods for KeystoneWebhook from zz_generated.deepcopy.go (lines 207â€“219 deleted).

resolution_summary: Both review comments were addressed exactly as suggested. The CEL XValidation rule on PolicyOverrides was changed from 'self.rules != null' to 'size(self.rules) > 0' to correctly reject empty maps and mirror the webhook's semantic validation. The KeystoneWebhook struct was annotated with '+kubebuilder:object:generate=false' and controller-gen was re-run, removing the unnecessary DeepCopyInto/DeepCopy methods from zz_generated.deepcopy.go.
